hypothesis: The reference topk recipe at K=128, EF=12, signum, LR=8e-4 should provide a clean first quality-anchor curve and runtime baseline; that anchor is needed before any LR or optimizer retuning can be interpreted.
summary: Run a clean mainline topk baseline at K=128 / EF=12 with signum and LR=8e-4 to establish the first curve-shape and runtime anchor for this session.
self_review: This round is calibration-first rather than improvement-first. It intentionally avoids introducing a confounded recipe change so the next LR move can be chosen from actual curve shape instead of guesswork.
notes_to_memory: Use this run as the clean topk curve and runtime anchor before any LR or optimizer comparison.
next_hypotheses: If the curve is stable but plateaus early, keep topk fixed and test a single-axis LR reduction to 6e-4.
If the curve is still improving late and remains smooth, keep topk fixed and test a single-axis LR increase to 1e-3 before changing optimizer.
